primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with concurrent AKI and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (WBC 14.2 K/uL) and low hemoglobin (9.8 g/dL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Progressive AKI is evident with creatinine rising from baseline to stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL), though the most extreme creatinine value was excluded as an outlier. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation criteria and suggests severe coagulopathy.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation to stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) meets KDIGO criteria despite outlier exclusion
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score criteria and indicates severe coagulopathy

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused imaging
Start vasopressor support if hypotension develops
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output
Consider platelet transfusion if thrombocytopenia worsens
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier for measurement error, assess for septic shock criteria (vasopressor requirement), and confirm infection source

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4
AKI stage 3 (Cr 8.9 excluded as outlier) with platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
